title: 28 Years Later: The Bone Temple Box Office Analysis
text: Director Nia DaCosta's sequel opened significantly below expectations. The film earned approximately $13 million domestically over its debut weekend, falling short of the anticipated $20 million. Globally, it pulled in $31.2 million against a similar budget to its predecessor. The contrast with the first installment is stark. The original 28 Years Later opened to $30 million domestically and eventually surpassed $150 million worldwide. This sequel's weaker start positions it poorly for profitability, as without exceptionally long legs, it's going to have a near impossible time breaking even for Sony. Despite receiving positive critical reviews, several elements may have hindered audience interest. The first film's pretty bizarre ending potentially discouraged some viewers. Releasing sequels just seven months apart may have been premature. Competition from other horror releases like Primate, Return to Silent Hill, and Send Help also factored into performance. A third installment featuring Cillian Murphy's return is already in development. The underperformance raises questions about whether Sony will adjust plans or proceed despite the financial struggles of the trilogy's second chapter.
